description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray coloration and a rough, uneven surface texture. The rock displays a mix of lighter and darker gray streaks and patches, suggesting a metamorphic or igneous origin. One side of the rock features a visible white patch with black handwritten numbers, indicating cataloging or identification for study or placement. The surface is jagged and angular, with some flatter areas that could potentially support small objects. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A dense, gray, irregularly shaped rock with rough, streaked surfaces and a visible catalog marking on one side. Its solid form and jagged edges make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element.
description_view_0: The main face of the rock is broad and shows a mottled gray surface with subtle streaks and rough texture.
description_view_1: This narrow side view highlights the rock's angular, pointed end and vertical striations.
description_view_2: The underside reveals a white patch with black handwritten numbers, contrasting with the dark gray, layered surface.
description_view_3: This view shows the elongated, sloped side of the rock with visible surface texture and a mix of gray tones.
